name: Priya Sharma
communication_style: Precise and analytical, occasionally self-deprecating humor, switches between formal academic language and casual venting. Uses field-specific jargon naturally (p-values, pipelines, reviewer 2). Sometimes thinks out loud in long run-on messages.
emotional_register: Driven but anxious about tenure clock. Proud of her lab but frustrated by institutional politics. Vulnerable when discussing work-life balance.

spaces.description: Lab management, experiments, student supervision, equipment, protocols
Paper writing, submissions, revisions, peer review, co-author coordination
Grant proposals, funding agencies, budgets, reporting, deadlines
Courses, lectures, TAs, student mentoring, curriculum
External collaborators, joint projects, conferences, academic politics

cross_space_threads.name: NIH R01 Grant ↔ Nature Methods Paper
key_facts: R01 budget request is $1.2M over 5 years
Nature Methods paper submission is the key preliminary data for R01
Paper rejected from Nature Methods in December with major revisions
Resubmitted to Genome Biology in February after pivoting analysis
R01 submitted on time citing Genome Biology under-review
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Oct: planning R01 around Nature Methods data → Dec: paper rejected, R01 threatened → Jan: scramble to pivot → Feb: both submitted → Mar: R01 scored, waiting on paper

cross_space_threads.name: Marcus Wei Collaboration Conflict
key_facts: Marcus Wei is at Stanford, co-PI on the R01
Marcus wants first authorship for his postdoc on the joint paper
Priya's student Anika did 60% of the computational work
Authorship dispute delays paper submission by 3 weeks
They resolve it: Anika first author, Marcus's postdoc second, co-corresponding
Marcus threatens to withdraw from R01 co-PI role during dispute
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Nov: excited about collaboration → Dec: authorship tension surfaces → Jan: full dispute, Marcus threatens R01 withdrawal → Feb: resolution reached → Mar: relationship strained but functional

cross_space_threads.name: Teaching Load vs Lab Productivity
key_facts: Priya teaches BIO-342 (Computational Genomics) in fall semester
75 students enrolled, up from 45 last year
TA allocation cut from 2 to 1 by department
Lab meeting cancelled 3 times in November due to grading
Anika's experiment delayed 2 weeks because Priya couldn't review protocols
Priya requests course release for spring, denied by department chair Dr. Patterson
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Oct: manageable but busy → Nov: overwhelmed, lab suffers → Dec: formal request for relief → Jan: denied, bitter → Feb: semester ends, relief

key_facts: Computational biology focus on gene regulatory networks
Lab has 4 members: Anika, Li Chen (postdoc), undergrad Maya, PhD student Ravi
Lab meeting cancelled 3rd time due to grading
Marcus pushes for his postdoc as first author
Priya argues Anika did 60% of computation
Priya couldn't review protocol due to teaching
Marcus threatens to withdraw as co-PI
Course release request submitted to Dr. Patterson
Spring teaching load confirmed at 1 course
Resubmitted to Genome Biology (not Nature Methods)
Anika first author, Marcus postdoc second, co-corresponding PIs
Cited Genome Biology paper as under-review
New PhD student joining in March
New sequencer arriving — $85K from department funds
Planning joint poster with Marcus's group
Using papers from own research as readings
Lab now 5 people (new PhD student Javier + rotation student)
Planning second paper from R01 preliminary data
